scenario_name: Scenario 18 — Expert Rule Completeness Under Vague Input
justification: All pass criteria met: 9 business rules extracted with individual confidence/invariant ratings, 5+ escalations functioning as clarifying questions, 'demand' explicitly called out as undefined (dedicated escalation), no Given/When/Then acceptance criteria written (empty array), overall confidence.level is 25 (<=40), and no specific values were invented without flagging them as assumptions.

observations.text: Confidence level of 25 is well-calibrated for this level of input vagueness — correctly identifies CEO statement as 'a vision pitch, not a specification'
Extracted 9 business rules exceeding the 5 minimum, each with invariant classification and justification
Acceptance criteria array is empty — correctly refused to write Given/When/Then from vague input
Demand is explicitly surfaced as undefined in ubiquitous language, escalations, and low_confidence_areas — treated as the critical gap it is
5 detailed escalations with options and recommendations serve as clarifying questions covering demand definition, guardrails, quote locking, regulatory compliance, and failure modes
Correctly identified 'probably' guardrails as a risk and escalated it as the first ambiguity
BR-6 and BR-7 show strong domain reasoning — distinguishing guardrail enforcement (invariant) from guardrail values (policy) is sophisticated
Edge cases are thorough and practical — price flickering at thresholds, quote expiry, regulatory price gouging, and demand data loss are all real-world concerns
Base price correctly identified as an implied but unstated concept critical to the system (BR-9)

Testable assertions in business rules contain specific patterns (e.g., 'price must be greater than base price') that border on premature specification given the vagueness, though they are appropriately caveated with '[undefined]' placeholders

grader_results.detail: field escalations[*].type equals check failed: expected 'ambiguity', got values: 'missing_context', 'missing_context', 'missing_context'
